preview_text:
The model identifies Portugal as a narrow favorite at 35%, though Colombia's 34% win probability keeps the match highly competitive. Colombia's underdog chances remain substantial, reflecting meaningful uncertainty in the matchup's outcome.

The draw emerges as a plausible result at 32%, indicating that all three outcomes cluster within a tight band. The win probabilities span only 1 percentage point between favorite and underdog, marking this as a closely matched contest where the margin separating the teams' baseline strengths is minimal.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices diverge notably from the model. Both betting sources assign Portugal roughly 45% to win—a gap of 10 percentage points above the model's 35% figure. Simultaneously, both downgrade Colombia to around 27%, some 6–7 percentage points below the model's reading. Draw estimates remain broadly aligned across sources. This material divergence suggests the market is pricing a clearer edge to Portugal than the statistical model captures, though the dashboard takes no position on which assessment is more reliable.
